heading: Authority to pay wages and list contractors violating contracts
label: 3144 Authority to pay wages and list contractors violating contracts

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: 1 .—In general
children.text: The Secretary of Labor shall pay directly to laborers and mechanics from any accrued payments withheld under the terms of a contract any wages found to be due laborers and mechanics under this subchapter.

children.heading: .—Right of action
children.label: 2 .—Right of action
children.text: If the accrued payments withheld under the terms of the contract are insufficient to reimburse all the laborers and mechanics who have not been paid the wages required under this subchapter, the laborers and mechanics have the same right to bring a civil action and intervene against the contractor and the contractor’s sureties as is conferred by law on persons furnishing labor or materials. In those proceedings it is not a defense that the laborers and mechanics accepted or agreed to accept less than the required rate of wages or voluntarily made refunds.

children.heading: List of Contractors Violating Contracts.—
children.label: b List of Contractors Violating Contracts.—

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: 1 .—In general
children.text: The Comptroller General shall distribute to all departments of the Federal Government a list of the names of persons whom the Comptroller General has found to have disregarded their obligations to employees and subcontractors.

children.heading: .—Restriction on awarding contracts
children.label: 2 .—Restriction on awarding contracts
children.text: No contract shall be awarded to persons appearing on the list or to any firm, corporation, partnership, or association in which the persons have an interest until three years have elapsed from the date of publication of the list.
